parsed_review.summary: CP00-925 is a descriptor-only RP30 P04 bridge pack (risk A, 10 implementation units, RP30.P04.M00.S03-RP30.P04.M01.S03) binding HROperations tail rows (7) into EmployeePortal foundation rows (3). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms registry.js, validators.js, model.test.js, contracts/hrx-people-contract.json, and scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs are internally consistent: pack binding, chain continuity (upstream CP00-924, next CP00-926/RP30.P04.M01.S04), and deliverable/micro-title distributions all match the baseline and prompt. All boundary flags remain closed (operations/portal runtime, record writes, policy execution, HR-state finalization, self-service actions, permission bypass, payroll runtime, HR AI final judgment, runtime receipts, real employee/candidate/payroll data, credentials/secrets, separate-product, enterprise-trust); a targeted grep for any of these set to true returned zero matches across registry, validators, and the contract JSON, and the contract validation marker is valid:true with a full no_write_attestation. Authority boundary is honored: the Claude packet is read_only and does not promote Claude to final approval, local validation does not claim enterprise trust, and the Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt=false used only for deterministic review/evidence handling rather than product runtime. Review-evidence hardening checks pass: readiness auth_preflight succeeded (exit 0, well-formed JSON, 1436-byte stdout), prompt is 2395B (<25600B), only Read/Grep/Glob tools were authorized, no forbidden tools, and no active untracked implementation files were omitted; UI-workstream dirty files (progress-control-room) are correctly classified as unrelated and out of pack scope. The pack-level production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false descriptor-verified claim is accurate and fully supported by implementation, tests, local validation, command evidence (no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false), inspection, and adjudication, all of which are consistently in the pending_review pre-closeout state. No P0/P1/P2 findings. One P3 informational note records that final pack closeout remains contingent on the post-review deterministic validation ladder and adjudication/receipt promotion that the artifacts themselves declare pending, and that this read-only review is independent verification, not final human/enterprise-trust approval. This receipt does not block pack closeout or this CP's goal closeout.

findings.title: Final closeout contingent on post-review deterministic ladder and adjudication (informational; not a defect)
findings.details: The pack's pre-review command-evidence (node --check; node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js; npm run rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate -- all exit_code 0) is a subset of the full baseline validation ladder. command-evidence.json:41 and adjudication.md explicitly state these checks must be re-confirmed by the post-promotion ladder (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) before commit, and that production_ready remains false until exactly one valid review receipt plus adjudication and queue regeneration complete. Consistently, construction-inspection.json has runtime_ready/status/boundary=null and claude-review-result.json is status=pending_review with null parsed_review. This is the documented descriptor-only pre-closeout process, not a code or boundary defect; it is recorded so the receipt makes explicit that this read-only review is independent verification and not final human/enterprise-trust approval.
findings.recommendation: Proceed as already specified: run the full deterministic validation ladder and complete adjudication plus review-receipt promotion at closeout, keeping production_ready/runtime_ready false and enterprise-trust closed until human final approval. No code or descriptor change is required.
